15 def run_fastapi(
16     agencies: Optional[List[Agency]] = None,
17     tools: Optional[List[Type[BaseTool]]] = None,
18     host: str = "0.0.0.0",
19     port: int = 8000,
20     app_token_env: str = "APP_TOKEN",
21     return_app: bool = False,
22     cors_origins: List[str] = None,
23 ):
24     """
25     Launch a FastAPI server exposing endpoints for multiple agencies and tools.
26     Each agency is deployed at /[agency-name]/get_completion and /[agency-name]/get_completion_stream.
27     Each tool is deployed at /tool/[tool-name].
28     """
29     if (agencies is None or len(agencies) == 0) and (tools is None or len(tools) == 0):
30         print("No endpoints to deploy. Please provide at least one agency or tool.")
31         return
32 
33     try:
34         import uvicorn
35         from fastapi import FastAPI
36         from fastapi.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware
37 
38         from .fastapi_utils.endpoint_handlers import (
39             exception_handler,
40             get_verify_token,
41             make_completion_endpoint,
42             make_stream_endpoint,
43             make_tool_endpoint,
44         )
45         from .fastapi_utils.request_models import BaseRequest, add_agent_validator
46     except ImportError:
47         print(
48             "FastAPI deployment dependencies are missing. Please install agency-swarm[fastapi] package"
49         )
50         return
51 
52     app_token = os.getenv(app_token_env)
53     if app_token is None or app_token == "":
54         print(f"Warning: {app_token_env} is not set. Authentication will be disabled.")
55     verify_token = get_verify_token(app_token)
56 
57     @asynccontextmanager
58     async def lifespan(app):
59         # Startup logic
60         global _EXECUTOR
61         from .fastapi_utils.endpoint_handlers import _EXECUTOR, _MAX_WORKERS
62         if _EXECUTOR is None:
63             print("Initializing ThreadPoolExecutor in FastAPI startup event")
64             _EXECUTOR = ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=_MAX_WORKERS)
65         else:
66             print("ThreadPoolExecutor already initialized")
67         try:
68             yield
69         finally:
70             # Shutdown logic
71             if _EXECUTOR is not None:
72                 print("Shutting down ThreadPoolExecutor in FastAPI shutdown event")
73                 _EXECUTOR.shutdown(wait=False, cancel_futures=True)
74                 _EXECUTOR = None
75             else:
76                 print("No ThreadPoolExecutor to shut down")
77 
78     app = FastAPI(lifespan=lifespan)
79     
80     app.add_middleware(
81         CORSMiddleware,
82         allow_origins=cors_origins or ["*"],
83         allow_credentials=True,
84         allow_methods=["*"],
85         allow_headers=["*"],
86     )
87 
88     endpoints = []
89     agency_names = []
90 
91     if agencies:
92         for idx, agency in enumerate(agencies):
93             agency_name = getattr(agency, "name", None)
94             if agency_name is None:
95                 agency_name = "agency" if len(agencies) == 1 else f"agency_{idx+1}"
96             agency_name = agency_name.replace(" ", "_")
97             if agency_name in agency_names:
98                 raise ValueError(
99                     f"Agency name {agency_name} is already in use. "
100                     "Please provide a unique name in the agency's 'name' parameter."
101                 )
102             agency_names.append(agency_name)
103 
104             # Store agent instances for easy lookup
105             AGENT_INSTANCES: Dict[str, "Agent"] = {
106                 agent.name: agent for agent in agency.agents
107             }
108 
109             class VerboseRequest(BaseRequest):
110                 verbose: bool = False
111 
112             AgencyRequest = add_agent_validator(VerboseRequest, AGENT_INSTANCES)
113             AgencyRequestStreaming = add_agent_validator(BaseRequest, AGENT_INSTANCES)
114 
115             app.add_api_route(
116                 f"/{agency_name}/get_completion",
117                 make_completion_endpoint(AgencyRequest, agency, verify_token),
118                 methods=["POST"],
119             )
120             app.add_api_route(
121                 f"/{agency_name}/get_completion_stream",
122                 make_stream_endpoint(AgencyRequestStreaming, agency, verify_token),
123                 methods=["POST"],
124             )
125             endpoints.append(f"/{agency_name}/get_completion")
126             endpoints.append(f"/{agency_name}/get_completion_stream")
127 
128     if tools:
129         for tool in tools:
130             tool_name = tool.__name__
131             tool_handler = make_tool_endpoint(tool, verify_token)
132             app.add_api_route(
133                 f"/tool/{tool_name}", tool_handler, methods=["POST"], name=tool_name
134             )
135             endpoints.append(f"/tool/{tool_name}")
136 
137     app.add_exception_handler(Exception, exception_handler)
138 
139     print("Created endpoints:\n" + "\n".join(endpoints))
140 
141     if return_app:
142         return app
143 
144     print(f"Starting FastAPI server at http://{host}:{port}")
145     
146     uvicorn.run(app, host=host, port=port)

84 
85 def run_mcp(
86     tools: Union[List[type[BaseTool]], str],
87     host: str = "0.0.0.0",
88     port: int = 8000,
89     app_token_env: str = "APP_TOKEN",
90     server_name: str = "mcp-tools-server",
91     cors_origins: List[str] = ["*"],
92     return_app: bool = False,
93 ):
94     """
95     Launch an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server exposing BaseTool instances.
96 
97     Args:
98         tools: List of BaseTool classes OR path to directory containing tool modules
99         host: Host to bind the server to. Only used if return_app is False.
100         port: Port to bind the server to. Only used if return_app is False.
101         app_token_env: Environment variable name for authentication token
102         server_name: Name identifier for the MCP server
103         cors_origins: List of allowed CORS origins
104         return_app: If False, runs the server automatically.
105         If True, return the Starlette app instead of running it.
106 
107     Returns:
108         Starlette app if return_app=True, otherwise None
109     """
110     
111     # Handle tools input - either list of classes or directory path
112     if isinstance(tools, str):
113         # It's a directory path
114         tools_list = _load_tools_from_directory(tools)
115         if not tools_list:
116             raise ValueError(f"No BaseTool classes found in directory: {tools}")
117         logger.info(f"Found {len(tools_list)} tools in {tools}")
118     else:
119         # It's a list of tool classes
120         tools_list = tools
121         if not tools_list or len(tools_list) == 0:
122             raise ValueError("No tools provided. Please provide at least one BaseTool class.")
123 
124     # Get authentication token
125     app_token = os.getenv(app_token_env)
126     if app_token is None or app_token == "":
127         logger.warning(f"{app_token_env} is not set. Authentication will be disabled.")
128 
129     # Create thread pool for sync tools
130     max_workers = int(os.getenv("TOOL_THREAD_POOL_SIZE", min(32, (os.cpu_count() or 1) + 4)))
131     if not os.getenv("TOOL_THREAD_POOL_SIZE"):
132         logger.warning(f"TOOL_THREAD_POOL_SIZE env variable is not set. Defaulting to {max_workers} max workers.")
133     thread_pool = ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=max_workers)
134 
135     # Create tool registry
136     tool_registry = {}
137 
138     # Register tools
139     for tool_class in tools_list:
140         tool_name = tool_class.__name__
141         if tool_name in tool_registry:
142             raise ValueError(f"Duplicate tool name detected: {tool_name}. Please use a different tool name.")
143         tool_registry[tool_name] = tool_class
144         logger.info(f"Registered tool: {tool_name}")
145 
146     def create_mcp_server() -> Server:
147         """Create MCP server with registered tools"""
148         app = Server(server_name)
149 
150         @app.call_tool()
151         async def call_tool(name: str, arguments: dict) -> list[types.TextContent]:
152             """Handle tool calls with proper error handling"""
153             try:
154                 # Find the registered tool
155                 if name not in tool_registry:
156                     logger.error(f"Unknown tool requested: {name}")
157                     return [
158                         types.TextContent(
159                             type="text",
160                             text=f"Error: Unknown tool '{name}'. Available tools: {list(tool_registry.keys())}",
161                         )
162                     ]
163 
164                 tool_class = tool_registry[name]
165 
166                 # Validate arguments against tool schema
167                 try:
168                     tool_instance = tool_class(**arguments)
169                 except Exception as e:
170                     logger.error(f"Invalid arguments for tool {name}: {e}")
171                     return [
172                         types.TextContent(type="text", text=f"Error: Invalid arguments for tool '{name}': {str(e)}")
173                     ]
174 
175                 # Execute tool
176                 if asyncio.iscoroutinefunction(tool_instance.run):
177                     result = await tool_instance.run()
178                 else:
179                     # Run sync tool in thread pool to avoid blocking
180                     loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()
181                     result = await loop.run_in_executor(thread_pool, tool_instance.run)
182 
183                 logger.info(f"Successfully executed tool: {name}")
184                 return [types.TextContent(type="text", text=str(result))]
185 
186             except Exception as e:
187                 logger.error(f"Error executing tool {name}: {e}", exc_info=True)
188                 return [types.TextContent(type="text", text=f"Error executing tool '{name}': {str(e)}")]
189 
190         @app.list_tools()
191         async def list_tools() -> list[types.Tool]:
192             """Generate tool list from registered BaseTool instances"""
193             tools_list = []
194 
195             try:
196                 for tool_name, tool_class in tool_registry.items():
197                     # Get the schema from the BaseTool
198                     schema = tool_class.model_json_schema()
199 
200                     # Get description from docstring
201                     description = tool_class.__doc__ or f"Tool: {tool_name}"
202                     description = description.strip()
203 
204                     # Create the MCP tool
205                     tools_list.append(types.Tool(name=tool_name, description=description, inputSchema=schema))
206 
207                 logger.info(f"Listed {len(tools_list)} available tools")
208                 return tools_list
209 
210             except Exception as e:
211                 logger.error(f"Error listing tools: {e}", exc_info=True)
212                 return []
213 
214         return app
215 
216     def create_app():
217         """Create the production-ready ASGI application"""
218         # Create MCP server
219         mcp_server = create_mcp_server()
220 
221         # Middleware stack
222         middleware = [
223             Middleware(
224                 CORSMiddleware,
225                 allow_origins=cors_origins,
226                 allow_credentials=True,
227                 allow_methods=["*"],
228                 allow_headers=["*"],
229             )
230         ]
231 
232         # Create session manager
233         session_manager = StreamableHTTPSessionManager(
234             app=mcp_server,
235             json_response=True,  # Use JSON for non-streaming
236         )
237 
238         async def handle_mcp(scope: Scope, receive: Receive, send: Send) -> None:
239             """Handle MCP requests with authentication"""
240             request = Request(scope, receive)
241 
242             # Simple token verification
243             if not _verify_token(request, app_token):
244                 response = JSONResponse(
245                     {"error": "Authentication required"}, status_code=401, headers={"WWW-Authenticate": "Bearer"}
246                 )
247                 await response(scope, receive, send)
248                 return
249 
250             try:
251                 await session_manager.handle_request(scope, receive, send)
252             except Exception as e:
253                 logger.error(f"Error handling MCP request: {e}", exc_info=True)
254                 response = JSONResponse({"error": "Internal server error"}, status_code=500)
255                 await response(scope, receive, send)
256 
257         @contextlib.asynccontextmanager
258         async def lifespan(app: Starlette) -> AsyncIterator[None]:
259             """Application lifespan management"""
260             try:
261                 async with session_manager.run():
262                     logger.info(f"MCP server '{server_name}' started")
263                     logger.info(f"Registered {len(tool_registry)} tools")
264                     logger.info(f"Authentication: {'Enabled' if app_token else 'Disabled'}")
265                     yield
266             except Exception as e:
267                 logger.error(f"Error during server startup: {e}", exc_info=True)
268                 raise
269             finally:
270                 logger.info("Shutting down MCP server...")
271                 # Shutdown thread pool to prevent thread leaks
272                 try:
273                     thread_pool.shutdown(wait=True)
274                     logger.info("Thread pool shut down successfully")
275                 except Exception as e:
276                     logger.error(f"Error shutting down thread pool: {e}", exc_info=True)
277 
278         # Fastapi interferes with session manager, so use Starlette directly
279         return Starlette(
280             routes=[
281                 Mount("/mcp", app=handle_mcp),
282             ],
283             lifespan=lifespan,
284             middleware=middleware,
285         )
286 
287     app = create_app()
288 
289     if return_app:
290         return app
291 
292     logger.info(f"MCP server running at http://{host}:{port}/mcp")
293 
294     try:
295         uvicorn.run(
296             app,
297             host=host,
298             port=port,
299         )
300     except KeyboardInterrupt:
301         logger.info("Server stopped by user")
302     except Exception as e:
303         logger.error(f"Server error: {e}", exc_info=True)
304         raise
